title: Colymbada elegantissima
intro: Colymbada elegantissima, also known as the elegant colymbadine, is a species of flowering plant in the dandelion family, Asteraceae. The health benefits of Colymbada elegantissima include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and exhibit antioxidant properties, making it a valuable asset in traditional medicine. The bioactive constituents of this plant include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Colymbada elegantissima include teas, infusions, and tinctures, often used to treat digestive issues and promote overall well-being.
taxonomy: Colymbada elegantissima is a species of flowering plant in the dandelion tribe within the sunflower family, known as Asteraceae. It is a perennial plant native to the Iberian Peninsula. Colymbada elegantissima is classified in the family Asteraceae, tribe Cichorieae, and genus Colymbada. The genus Colymbada is sometimes considered to be part of the genus Crepis. However, some sources still recognize the genus Colymbada as a distinct group.
